seo.title: PII Filtering for AI Agents in Flowise Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix pii filtering for ai agents in Flowise agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In Flowise, PII Filtering for AI Agents manifests as unfiltered personal data leaking through chatflow chains, vector store retrievals, and custom tool nodes due to the platform's drag-and-drop nature that bypasses traditional validation layers. Agents often process sensitive user inputs without regex or NER-based scrubbing, especially in multi-agent handoffs where context flows unchecked between nodes. This creates silent PII exposure in RAG pipelines and conversational flows, amplifying risks in production deployments.

howItManifests.scenario: Chatflow chains producing unvalidated intermediate outputs
howItManifests.symptom: LLM Response Parser nodes output raw user PII from previous Conversation Chain nodes without filtering
howItManifests.impact: Customer emails/phone numbers appear in agent responses and logs

howItManifests.scenario: Vector store retrieval returning low-relevance documents
howItManifests.symptom: Document Loader nodes ingest unscrubbed PDFs containing PII that gets retrieved by Vector Store Retriever
howItManifests.impact: Historical customer data exposed in unrelated queries

howItManifests.scenario: Custom tool nodes failing silently without error propagation
howItManifests.symptom: Custom Function Tool nodes process PII inputs but return unfiltered results to agent
howItManifests.impact: API keys and credentials passed through tools without validation

howItManifests.scenario: Multi-agent sequential flow losing context between agent handoffs
howItManifests.symptom: Agent handoff between Conversational Agent and Zero-Shot Agent drops PII filtering between chains
howItManifests.impact: PII accumulates across agent conversations without cleanup

detection.name: Chatflow Intermediate Output Monitor
detection.description: Injects into Flowise callback system to scan all node outputs for PII patterns using regex and Flowise's execution context

detection.name: Custom Tool Input Scanner
detection.description: Wraps Flowise Custom Tool nodes to validate inputs before execution and log PII attempts

detection.name: Vector Store PII Audit
detection.description: Scans Flowise vector store embeddings for PII contamination during retrieval

fixes.name: PII Scrubber Middleware Node
fixes.description: Custom Flowise node that sits between all LLM chains to automatically redact PII before downstream processing

fixes.name: Safe Agent Handoff Wrapper
fixes.description: Wraps multi-agent flows to ensure PII is scrubbed during context handoffs between agents

fixes.name: Document Preprocessing Pipeline
fixes.description: Automatically scrubs PII from documents before they enter Flowise vector stores

vexAutomation.heading: Automating PII Filtering for AI Agents Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex integrates directly as a callback handler in Flowise chatflow configuration, automatically monitoring every node execution for PII leakage across chat chains, vector retrievals, and agent handoffs. It employs production-grade NER models and regex patterns tuned for Flowise's execution patterns, providing real-time blocking and remediation without modifying core flow logic. 

The Vex SDK captures Flowise's callback events (onChainStart, onRetrieverEnd, onToolStart) and runs them through its PII detection pipeline, automatically suggesting and applying fixes like middleware insertion or document preprocessing. For multi-agent flows, Vex maintains session-level PII tracking across handoffs, ensuring comprehensive coverage that Flowise's native logging misses.

Deployment takes seconds via npm install @vex_dev/sdk, with Vex automatically discovering all PII exposure points and generating remediation PRs to your Flowise flow definitions.

cta.heading: Detect and Fix This Automatically with Vex
cta.description: Install Vex in 30 seconds to automatically secure all your Flowise agents against PII leakage.
